context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Left

episode_text:
day=9; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Watching TV with TV on Left; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., watching TV on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated left-attention meals with these foods showed the user explicitly preferred hotter food. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated front-TV lunch-style meals showed a preference for substantially hotter food. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simpler non-sauced meal without the observed lunch-style complexity signal THEN 1 min BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals also supported the quieter default in some non-dip cases. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed right-TV fruit/dessert meal preserved the intended cold serving temperature. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN no microwave BECAUSE the new left-TV fruit breakfast also shows the user prefers fruit served cold rather than reheated.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or visual focus in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated forward-attention meals show the user prefers preserving the forward view. IF dining context includes a TV, laptop, or other visual focus on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or plain fruit THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE multiple left-attention meals, including the new fruit meal, show the user explicitly chose to preserve the left-side view. IF dining context includes a TV or other sustained visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food includes fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the observed right-attention meal shows the user wants the robot to protect the active viewing side on the right.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or plain fruit THEN slow BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently shifted away from fast motion, and the new left-TV fruit meal strengthens a stable preference for gentler movement when attention is shared with a left-side activity. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front (e.g., watching TV in front) AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN slow or medium, with evidence favoring slower motion for more involved lunch foods BECAUSE front-TV meals with chicken-and-broccoli and bite-sized sandwiches used medium and slow rather than fast. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simpler non-sauced meal without the observed lunch-style complexity signal THEN fast BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals supported efficient motion in simpler forward-attention contexts. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN fast BECAUSE the observed right-TV meal shows the user tolerated quick motion in that context.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND food is a general meal item without evidence of bite-shape concerns THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this was selected across most observed meals, indicating a general preference for reliable bite acquisition. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the observed sandwich meal shows the user accepted a narrower-bite strategy for that food type. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is soft elongated fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the new left-TV fruit meal shows the user preferred the narrower-bite orientation for these fruit items.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN yes BECAUSE earlier left-attention meals with messier foods show the user wanted extra verification. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-TV lunch-style meals show the user also wants extra confirmation for some non-dip entrées. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN no BECAUSE the observed right-TV meal shows the user accepted a lower-interaction workflow in that context. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain fruit without sauce or dip THEN no BECAUSE the new left-TV fruit meal shows the user did not want extra confirmation for a simple cold fruit breakfast. IF dining context is quiet, social/front-attention, or front-TV AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special messiness or lunch-style complexity signal THEN no BECAUSE several earlier simpler meals supported a lower-interaction workflow when extra verification was not needed.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user's lack of trunk control prevents leaning forward comfortably, and all observed selections are consistent with this physical constraint.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer in any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside mouth transfer, which is incompatible with the user's physical limitations and unsupported by observed selections.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or plain fruit THEN speech + LED BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently show the user prefers a more salient multimodal cue while attention is divided, and the new fruit meal extends this pattern beyond messy foods. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated front-TV lunch-style meals indicate the user wants stronger cues when engaged with forward media during more involved foods. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip and no added complexity signal THEN LED BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the simpler visual cue. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the observed right-TV meal shows the user was satisfied with the simpler visual-only cue in that context.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across social, front-attention, left-attention, and right-attention meals. This indicates a stable preference for minimizing effort over more explicit initiation methods.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across meals, indicating a stable preference for minimizing effort.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF any observed dining context AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user consistently chose low-effort automatic initiation across meals, indicating a stable preference for minimizing effort.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or plain fruit THEN speech + LED BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently show the user preferred a more noticeable multimodal completion cue while attention was divided, and the new fruit meal extends this pattern beyond messy foods. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entrée or sandwich-style meal without dipping THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated front-TV lunch-style meals indicate the user wants stronger completion cues in that context. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip and no added complexity signal THEN LED BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the simpler visual completion cue. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the observed right-TV meal shows the user was satisfied with the simpler visual-only completion cue in that context.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND feeding transfer occurs THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, and button use is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND drinking transfer occurs THEN perception or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN perception or autocontinue have both been selected BECAUSE front-TV meals show a stable low-effort preference with context-specific flexibility between passive sensing and timeout-based progression. IF dining context is quiet, left-attention, or right-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred BECAUSE observed choices remain within perception and autocontinue rather than explicit control, with the new left-TV fruit meal specifically supporting autocontinue and the right-TV meal supporting perception.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND mouth wiping occurs THEN perception or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip THEN perception or autocontinue have both been selected BECAUSE front-TV meals show a stable low-effort preference with flexibility in how completion is recognized. IF dining context is quiet, left-attention, or right-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred BECAUSE observed choices remain within perception and autocontinue rather than explicit control, with the new left-TV fruit meal specifically supporting autocontinue and the right-TV meal supporting perception.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to external media or work (e.g., TV on the left, laptop on the left, TV in front, or TV on the right) AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN yes BECAUSE across observed media-attention meals, including the new left-TV fruit breakfast, the user chose retraction to reduce visual obstruction while attending to a screen. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN no BECAUSE the user accepted less interruption and shorter meal time in that meal.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no sauce or dip available AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping in that meal, consistent with minimizing mess in social settings. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE observed left-attention meals show the user preferred some dipping but not heavy dipping. IF food includes cream, sauce, or a dippable topping AND dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the observed right-TV fruit meal also shows a preference for limited rather than maximal sauce application. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain fruit without sauce or dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE the new left-TV fruit meal confirms the user does not add dipping when it is unnecessary.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left (e.g., TV on the left or laptop on the left) AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with sauce THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE earlier left-attention meals show a strong preference for ample time to intervene when attention is divided and the food may be messier. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain fruit without sauce or dip THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the new left-TV fruit meal shows the user preferred quick progression for a simple cold breakfast despite divided attention. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory mixed entrée without dipping THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE one front-TV entrée meal showed the user wanted a very long intervention window for that more involved lunch food. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches without dipping THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed front-TV sandwich meal shows the user preferred quick progression for that easier handoff pattern. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed right-TV meal shows the user preferred quick progression despite divided attention. IF dining context is social with a person in front or includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special complexity signal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals used the shortest wait, indicating preference for quick progression in simpler contexts.
